evidence_excerpt: His motor disability progressed to marked distal weakness, wasting, and contractures; by age 8 he could walk short distances but required a wheelchair for longer journeys.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Progressive motor disability with marked distal weakness, wasting, contractures, and sensory deficits.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: From 2 years of age neurologic involvement became evident. He had frequent falls, secondary to foot drop.
His motor disability progressed to marked distal weakness, wasting, and contractures.
At 7 years of age he underwent triple arthrodesis.
At 8 years of age he could walk short distances but required a wheelchair for longer journeys.

notes_on_uncertainty: Index time taken as neonatal diagnosis; follow-up details extend to approximately age 8 with no data beyond that. Degree of benefit from orthopedic surgery is not specified.
